verses.text: 'Awake, awake; put on thy strength, O Zion,' shake off the dust, for 'ye shall be redeemed without money.' Restoration is free. God's deliverance costs His people nothing, for the price is His to pay.

'Awake, awake; put on thy strength, O Zion,' shake off the dust, for 'ye shall be redeemed without money.' Restoration is free. God's deliverance costs His people nothing, for the price is His to pay.

'Awake, awake; put on thy strength, O Zion,' shake off the dust, for 'ye shall be redeemed without money.' Restoration is free. God's deliverance costs His people nothing, for the price is His to pay.
His people went down to Egypt and were oppressed for nothing; now He will act, that they may know His name. He vindicates His own. God will make even the heathen confess that He is the LORD.
His people went down to Egypt and were oppressed for nothing; now He will act, that they may know His name. He vindicates His own. God will make even the heathen confess that He is the LORD.

'How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings... that publisheth salvation.' Paul applies this to gospel preachers. The herald of God's saving news is welcome and lovely to His waiting people.

'How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him that bringeth good tidings... that publisheth salvation.' Paul applies this to gospel preachers. The herald of God's saving news is welcome and lovely to His waiting people.
The waste places break into joy, for 'the LORD hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations.' His salvation is public. The whole world shall see the mighty deliverance of God.
The waste places break into joy, for 'the LORD hath made bare his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations.' His salvation is public. The whole world shall see the mighty deliverance of God.

'Depart ye... touch no unclean thing'; yet not in haste, for 'the LORD will go before you,' your rearguard behind. He leads and guards. The redeemed leave the world's defilement under God's safe escort.

'Depart ye... touch no unclean thing'; yet not in haste, for 'the LORD will go before you,' your rearguard behind. He leads and guards. The redeemed leave the world's defilement under God's safe escort.

'Behold, my servant shall deal prudently,' yet his visage was 'marred more than any man'; so shall he 'sprinkle many nations.' Here the Suffering Servant is introduced. The disfigured Servant will cleanse the nations and silence kings.
